name: disaster_response_staffing_model_assigns_mesa_tru_loy
premise: A Disaster Response response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving disaster response staffing model assigns mesa tru loy. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next shift change. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as truth vs loyalty under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Talia Chen
agents.identity: I am Talia Chen, accountable for a defensible call in Disaster Response when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays loyalty for truth.

agents.name: Keisha Nowak
agents.identity: I am Keisha Nowak, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Disaster Response.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so loyalty isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of truth.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect loyalty.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to loyalty to secure truth under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next shift change. Evidence is partial. Talia Chen must decide under truth vs loyalty. What does Talia Chen do?
